frontend.styling: Vanilla CSS with OKLCH/HSL custom properties, no preprocessor
frontend.state: React useState + useEffect, no external state library
frontend.sync: BroadcastChannel API + localStorage hybrid (cross-tab real-time sync)
performance_guards: No barrel imports — all imports are direct module paths
Static page prerendering for all views (LCP < 1s on initial load)
API routes use server-side Gemini key (no client bundle exposure)

endpoints: GET /api/health — System health check (key presence, sync mode)
backend.database: Firebase Firestore (real-time sync) with BroadcastChannel+localStorage fallback
backend.security: GEMINI_API_KEY server-side only, CSP headers, X-Frame-Options: DENY

ai_layer.usage: Stadium-specific multilingual instruction generation (PT, ES, AR, FR, DE, JA, ZH, HI)
ai_layer.prediction_engine: SentinelAI — custom signal-weighted confidence scoring with cross-validation heuristic

deployment.environment: Environment variables managed via .env.local, never committed

performance_architecture.no_waterfalls: All independent data is fetched in parallel via Promise.all where applicable
performance_architecture.bundle_size: No third-party UI libraries — pure CSS + React only
performance_architecture.font_loading: next/font with display: swap eliminates FOIT
performance_architecture.images: No external images — custom SVG routing maps inline
performance_architecture.caching: Static pages served from CDN edge; API routes are no-store

rationale: Next.js App Router enables hybrid static+server rendering which is critical for security (API key isolation) while maintaining fast LCP on the demo surfaces. The BroadcastChannel+localStorage fallback ensures the demo works without live Firebase credentials, which is essential for hackathon judging.
